meaning: adj.使人神魂颠倒的,使人入神的
examples: I might never have heard that entrancing sound, or smelt that bewitching smell!
In the Philippines, an entrancing healer, allegedly, has the ability to materialize and dematerialize matter.
Some entrancing healers can pull out molars with their bare hands, while others can remove and replace eyeballs.
He cut a dashing figure in his fatigues and red beret, and peasants at all-night rallies found his oratory entrancing.
We can give him that, but his playing is entrancing whether you see it as part of history or in a vacuum.
